FINAL JUDGMENT AND ORDER OF DISTRIBUTION

IN THE CIRCUIT COURT OF COOK COUNTY, ILLINOIS
PROBATE DIVISION

In Re: Estate of MARGARET ELEANOR WORTHINGTON, Deceased
Case No. 2023-P-004521

THIS CAUSE came before the Court on the Petition for Final Distribution filed by First National Bank of Chicago, as Executor of the Estate of Margaret Eleanor Worthington ("Decedent"), who died testate on October 12, 2023, at the age of 87, at Northwestern Memorial Hospital, 251 East Huron Street, Chicago, Illinois 60611.

The Court, having reviewed the final accounting, the inventory and appraisal, all claims filed against the estate, and the objections filed by interested parties, and being fully advised in the premises, FINDS as follows:

I. ESTATE ASSETS AND VALUATION

The total gross estate of the Decedent, as determined by the appraiser, Robert J. Flanagan, MAI, of Flanagan & Associates, 180 North LaSalle Street, Suite 3200, Chicago, Illinois 60601, is valued at $14,850,000.00, consisting of:

(a) Real Property:
  - Residence at 1440 North Lake Shore Drive, Unit 32B, Chicago, IL 60610 — $3,200,000.00
  - Vacation home at 285 Ocean Road, Kennebunkport, Maine 04046 — $1,750,000.00
  - Rental property at 742 West Webster Avenue, Chicago, IL 60614 — $825,000.00

(b) Financial Assets:
  - JPMorgan Chase & Co. investment account ending in 4589 — $4,250,000.00
  - Charles Schwab & Co. brokerage account ending in 7823 — $2,100,000.00
  - Northern Trust Company trust account — $1,450,000.00
  - Bank of America checking account ending in 3012 — $175,000.00

(c) Personal Property:
  - Art collection appraised by Christie's International Real Estate — $850,000.00
  - Jewelry appraised by Tiffany & Co. — $250,000.00

II. DEBTS AND EXPENSES

The following claims have been allowed:
  - Northwestern Memorial Hospital — medical expenses: $287,450.00
  - Internal Revenue Service — federal estate tax (Form 706): $2,145,000.00
  - Illinois Department of Revenue — state estate tax: $412,000.00
  - Executor's fees (First National Bank of Chicago): $148,500.00
  - Attorney's fees (Winston & Strawn LLP): $95,000.00
  - Accounting fees (Deloitte & Touche LLP): $45,000.00
  - Funeral and burial expenses: $22,500.00
Total debts and expenses: $3,155,450.00

III. NET DISTRIBUTABLE ESTATE

The net distributable estate, after payment of all allowed claims and expenses, is $11,694,550.00.

IV. DISTRIBUTION

Pursuant to the Last Will and Testament of Margaret Eleanor Worthington, executed on June 3, 2019, and admitted to probate on November 15, 2023, the estate shall be distributed as follows:

(a) To ELIZABETH ANNE WORTHINGTON PRICE, daughter, of 890 Park Avenue, New York, NY 10075 — 40% of the net estate: $4,677,820.00

(b) To THOMAS JAMES WORTHINGTON, son, of 1200 Pacific Coast Highway, Malibu, CA 90265 — 40% of the net estate: $4,677,820.00

(c) To THE UNIVERSITY OF CHICAGO, for the Margaret Worthington Memorial Scholarship Fund, 5801 South Ellis Avenue, Chicago, IL 60637 — 10% of the net estate: $1,169,455.00

(d) To THE ART INSTITUTE OF CHICAGO, 111 South Michigan Avenue, Chicago, IL 60603 — 5% of the net estate: $584,727.50

(e) To CATHOLIC CHARITIES OF THE ARCHDIOCESE OF CHICAGO, 721 North LaSalle Drive, Chicago, IL 60654 — 5% of the net estate: $584,727.50

V. ORDER

IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that the Executor shall distribute the estate in accordance with the foregoing findings within sixty (60) days of the date of this Order. The Executor shall file a receipt of distribution with this Court upon completion.

The claim filed by DAVID MICHAEL WORTHINGTON, nephew, of 445 East Ohio Street, Chicago, IL 60611, contesting the validity of the Will under 755 ILCS 5/4-1, is DENIED. The Court finds that the Decedent had testamentary capacity and was not subject to undue influence. See In re Estate of Hoover, 155 Ill. 2d 402 (1993); In re Estate of Henke, 203 Ill. App. 3d 975 (1990).

ENTERED this 1st day of March, 2024.

Hon. Patricia M. Sullivan
Judge of the Circuit Court of Cook County
Richard J. Daley Center
50 West Washington Street
Chicago, Illinois 60602

concepts.folio_definition: A Judgment is a written decision by a court or other tribunal determining the rights and obligations of the parties involved in a legal dispute, often following a trial or other legal proceeding.
A Summary Judgment is a legal decision made by a judge based on the evidence and facts presented in a case, without the need for a trial.
An Offer of Judgment is a legal document submitted by one party to another during a lawsuit, proposing a settlement amount to resolve the case.
A stipulated judgment is a legal decision agreed upon by both parties involved in a case, which is then approved by a judge.

A judgment lien is a type of lien that is created when a court grants a judgment in favor of a creditor, giving them the right to place a lien on the debtor's property in order to secure payment of the debt.
The Entry of Judgment is a legal event that marks the official recording of a court or arbitral decision, which may include a judgment or a decree.

concepts.folio_definition: An Order, also known as a Ruling, is a written document issued by a trial court that directs parties to take certain actions or makes a decision on a specific matter in a legal case.
A judicial order is a written directive issued by a court, typically in response to a motion or petition, that directs a party to take or refrain from taking a particular action.
A Protective Order is a legal order issued by a court to protect a person or entity from harm or harassment by another party.

An Order for Telephonic Hearing is a written document that directs a court proceeding to be conducted via telephone, videoconference, or other remote means, and is typically used in bankruptcy and restructuring cases.

A Work Order Clause is a provision in a contract that outlines the specific tasks or services to be performed by one party for another, often including details such as timelines, deliverables, and compensation.
A Temporary Restraining Order (TRO) is a provisional legal remedy that prohibits a party from taking certain actions until a court can make a final decision on the matter.

concepts.folio_definition: The concept of Disbursement (Distribution) refers to the act of distributing funds or assets to the intended recipients, often as part of a larger event or phase.

A Mixed Distribution Fund is an investment fund that allocates its investment profits by partially distributing them to shareholders and partially reinvesting them back into the fund. This balanced approach aims to provide both income and capital growth to investors.

This industry comprises: (1) establishments primarily engaged in operating gas distribution systems (e.g., mains, meters); (2) establishments known as gas marketers that buy gas from the well and sell it to a distribution system; (3) establishments known as gas brokers or agents that arrange the sale of gas over gas distribution systems operated by others; and (4) establishments primarily engaged in transmitting and distributing gas to final consumers.

This industry comprises: (1) establishments primarily engaged in operating gas distribution systems (e.g., mains, meters); (2) establishments known as gas marketers that buy gas from the well and sell it to a distribution system; (3) establishments known as gas brokers or agents that arrange the sale of gas over gas distribution systems operated by others; and (4) establishments primarily engaged in transmitting and distributing gas to final consumers.

Asset Distribution Policy refers to the strategy a fund employs to manage and allocate its investment profits. This policy includes income funds, which regularly distribute profits to investors; accumulation funds, which reinvest profits back into the fund; and mixed funds, which combine both strategies by partially distributing and partially reinvesting profits.

A court of general jurisdiction in the state of Georgia that has authority over civil and criminal cases within Cook County.
Minnesota District Court - Cook County: A specific court within the Minnesota District Court system that has jurisdiction over legal matters within Cook County, Minnesota.
Illinois Circuit Court is a court of general jurisdiction in the state of Illinois that hears civil and criminal cases.
The Georgia Superior Court is a court of general jurisdiction in the state of Georgia that has the power to hear and decide a wide range of civil and criminal cases.

concepts.folio_definition: A trust, in estate planning, is a legal entity that holds and manages assets for the benefit of specific individuals or entities, known as beneficiaries. It's established by a grantor (or settlor) and is overseen by a trustee who ensures the assets are managed and distributed according to the grantor's instructions.
A business trust is a form of business organization which is similar to a corporation, in which investors receive transferable certificates of beneficial interest.

A Living Trust, also known as a revocable trust, is a legal entity created during the grantor's lifetime to hold and manage assets. It can be altered, amended, or revoked by the grantor at any time before their death, allowing flexibility in asset management and distribution.

An Irrevocable Trust is a legal entity that, once established, cannot be altered, amended, or revoked by the grantor without the consent of the beneficiaries. Assets transferred into this trust are permanently removed from the grantor's estate, offering potential tax benefits and protection from creditors.
A Deed of Trust is a legal agreement that transfers the title of real property to a trustee as security for a loan between a borrower and a lender.

Trust and Estate Claims refer to legal actions related to disputes over the distribution of assets and property left behind by a deceased person, including challenges to the validity of wills, allegations of mismanagement by trustees, and interference with expected inheritances.

concepts.folio_definition: A trust, in estate planning, is a legal entity that holds and manages assets for the benefit of specific individuals or entities, known as beneficiaries. It's established by a grantor (or settlor) and is overseen by a trustee who ensures the assets are managed and distributed according to the grantor's instructions.
A business trust is a form of business organization which is similar to a corporation, in which investors receive transferable certificates of beneficial interest.

A Living Trust, also known as a revocable trust, is a legal entity created during the grantor's lifetime to hold and manage assets. It can be altered, amended, or revoked by the grantor at any time before their death, allowing flexibility in asset management and distribution.

An Irrevocable Trust is a legal entity that, once established, cannot be altered, amended, or revoked by the grantor without the consent of the beneficiaries. Assets transferred into this trust are permanently removed from the grantor's estate, offering potential tax benefits and protection from creditors.
A Deed of Trust is a legal agreement that transfers the title of real property to a trustee as security for a loan between a borrower and a lender.

Trust and Estate Claims refer to legal actions related to disputes over the distribution of assets and property left behind by a deceased person, including challenges to the validity of wills, allegations of mismanagement by trustees, and interference with expected inheritances.

concepts.folio_definition: A bank is a financial institution licensed to receive deposits, make loans, and provide various financial services such as wealth management, currency exchange, and safe deposit boxes. Banks play a crucial role in the economy by facilitating financial transactions, providing credit, and offering investment products.

The Export/Import Bank of the United States (abbreviated as Ex-Im Bank or the Bank) is the official export credit agency (ECA) of the United States federal government. Operating as a wholly owned federal government corporation, the Bank "assists in financing and facilitating U.S. exports of goods and services".
Bank Mishandling Funds Transfer refers to a legal claim made by a creditor against a bank for improperly handling the transfer of funds, which may involve commercial paper and banking regulations.

When a bank fails to complete a funds transfer initiated by a customer before the customer closes their account or withdraws their authorization, resulting in the funds not being transferred, and potentially causing financial harm to the customer.

concepts.folio_label: Bank’s Wrongful Payment of Negotiable Instrument
concepts.folio_definition: The Bank's Wrongful Payment of Negotiable Instrument refers to a situation where a bank makes a payment on a negotiable instrument without proper authorization or without verifying the authenticity of the instrument, resulting in a loss to the rightful owner of the instrument.

concepts.folio_label: Bank Misconduct When Relying on Power of Attorney
concepts.folio_definition: Bank Misconduct When Relying on Power of Attorney: The wrongful or illegal actions taken by a bank when it relies on a power of attorney granted by a customer to conduct transactions on their behalf, often resulting in harm or loss to the customer.

Accounts can come in various forms, such as savings accounts, checking accounts, or investment accounts, each with their own rules, benefits, and limitations.
Financial and operational indicators designed to evaluate the performance of engagements or transactions.

A Payable-on-Death (POD) Account is a financial account, such as a bank account or a certificate of deposit, that allows the account holder to designate a specific beneficiary who will receive the funds in the account upon the account holder's death. This designation operates outside of the probate process, making it a straightforward way to transfer assets to a chosen beneficiary without the need for a will or going through the probate court.
A European Unit of Account 9 (E.U.A.-9) is a bond market unit that falls under the Currency category and is used as a standardization of money in the European financial market.

Often considered in Bankruptcy Proceedings, Personal Goods are movable, tangible objects owned by an individual and primarily used for personal, household, or everyday living purposes. They are distinct from real property (real estate) and intellectual property.

Financial Obligations (Financial Liabilities) refer to any monetary commitments or liabilities an individual or entity is responsible for, such as paying off debts, fulfilling contract terms, or covering recurring expenses. concepts.folio_definition: An Estate refers to all the possessions, property, and legal rights owned by an individual, including land, tenements, and personal assets. Real estate office buildings.

An Airport - Real Estate Asset refers to the land, buildings, and infrastructure associated with an airport, including runways, terminals, and other facilities. Estate Planning Objectives refer to the goals and purposes that individuals aim to achieve when organizing their assets, properties, and financial affairs for the future distribution upon their death. These objectives ensure the proper management and transfer of wealth, fulfillment of personal wishes, and minimization of taxes and legal complications.

concepts.folio_label: Bank’s Wrongful Payment of Negotiable Instrument

concepts.folio_label: Bank Misconduct When Relying on Power of Attorney
concepts.folio_definition: Bank Misconduct When Relying on Power of Attorney: The wrongful or illegal actions taken by a bank when it relies on a power of attorney granted by a customer to conduct transactions on their behalf, often resulting in harm or loss to the customer.

concepts.folio_definition: A lawyer is a legal professional who provides legal advice and representation to clients in various legal matters, including litigation, transactions, and regulatory compliance.

A billing attorney is the lawyer responsible for keeping track of billable hours and expenses associated with a legal matter, and for preparing and issuing invoices to clients. This attorney ensures that the client is accurately billed for legal services provided, and may also address any billing inquiries or disputes that may arise.

A responsible attorney is the lawyer who is designated as the primary person in charge of overseeing and managing a specific legal matter or case. This attorney is accountable for the progress, strategy, communication, and overall handling of the case, and may delegate tasks to other attorneys or support staff under their supervision.

A relationship attorney is a lawyer who serves as the primary point of contact and maintains an ongoing relationship with a client on behalf of a law firm. This attorney is responsible for understanding the client's needs, expectations, and preferences, and works to ensure that the firm provides consistent, high-quality legal services tailored to the client's requirements.
The intentional misrepresentation or concealment of facts by an attorney to their client or the court, which constitutes a breach of the attorney's duty of loyalty and candor.

The Attorney-Client Privilege is a legal concept that protects the confidentiality of communications between an attorney and their client, and prevents those communications from being disclosed without the client's consent.

concepts.folio_definition: Full Payment refers to a financial transaction where the total amount due has been paid in its entirety. This status indicates that the current financial obligation has been completely satisfied, leaving no outstanding balance.

Goods and Services Tax Payment encompasses all taxes and duties levied on the production, extraction, sale, transfer, leasing, or delivery of goods, as well as the rendering of services. This also includes taxes on the use of goods or permissions to use goods or perform activities, ensuring comprehensive taxation of economic activities related to goods and services.
folio_alt_labels: Payment of Tax on Goods and Services

concepts.folio_definition: Sales and Consumption Tax Payment (aka Tax payment on production, sale, transfer, leasing and delivery of goods and rendering of services) consists of all taxes, levied on transactions in goods and services on the basis of their intrinsic characteristics (e.g., value, weight of tobacco, strength of alcohol, and so on) as distinct from taxes imposed on the use of goods, or permission to use goods or perform activities.
folio_alt_labels: Tax payment on production, sale, transfer, leasing and delivery of goods and rendering of services

concepts.folio_label: General Sales and Consumption Tax Payment
concepts.folio_definition: General Sales and Consumption Tax Payment includes all taxes, other than import and export duties, levied on the production, leasing, transfer, delivery, or sale of a wide range of goods and the rendering of a wide range of services. These taxes apply to both domestically produced and imported goods, irrespective of the stage of production or distribution at which they are levied. This category covers value-added taxes, sales taxes, and multi-stage cumulative taxes, including adjustments for border transactions.

Payment of public benefit refers to the disbursement of financial assistance or provision of services to eligible recipients under a specific public benefit program. Payments can be made through various methods such as direct deposit, checks, or electronic benefit transfer (EBT) cards, and are generally subject to periodic reviews or re-certifications to confirm ongoing eligibility

concepts.folio_label: Lump Sum Payment of Public Benefit
concepts.folio_definition: Lump sum payment of public benefit refers to the disbursement of financial assistance in a single, large payment rather than through ongoing, smaller payments. This approach is often used as part of a diversion objective to encourage recipients to use the funds for specific purposes like securing housing or job training, with the goal of reducing long-term dependency on public benefits.

A Joint Will is a single testamentary document executed by two or more individuals, typically spouses, which combines their respective wills into one instrument. In the U.S., Medicare is often cited as an example, though it primarily serves individuals 65 and older or those with specific disabilities, rather than the entire population.

Trust and Estate Claims refer to legal actions related to disputes over the distribution of assets and property left behind by a deceased person, including challenges to the validity of wills, allegations of mismanagement by trustees, and interference with expected inheritances.

Estate Planning Objectives refer to the goals and purposes that individuals aim to achieve when organizing their assets, properties, and financial affairs for the future distribution upon their death. These objectives ensure the proper management and transfer of wealth, fulfillment of personal wishes, and minimization of taxes and legal complications.

concepts.folio_definition: An Order, also known as a Ruling, is a written document issued by a trial court that directs parties to take certain actions or makes a decision on a specific matter in a legal case.
A judicial order is a written directive issued by a court, typically in response to a motion or petition, that directs a party to take or refrain from taking a particular action.
A Protective Order is a legal order issued by a court to protect a person or entity from harm or harassment by another party.

An Order for Telephonic Hearing is a written document that directs a court proceeding to be conducted via telephone, videoconference, or other remote means, and is typically used in bankruptcy and restructuring cases.

A Work Order Clause is a provision in a contract that outlines the specific tasks or services to be performed by one party for another, often including details such as timelines, deliverables, and compensation.
A Temporary Restraining Order (TRO) is a provisional legal remedy that prohibits a party from taking certain actions until a court can make a final decision on the matter.

concepts.folio_definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

A claim brought under the Antiterrorism Act, which allows individuals to seek damages for injuries caused by acts of international terrorism, including acts committed by foreign governments or organizations.

A speeding violation claim is an assertion made by a law enforcement officer or traffic camera that a driver has exceeded the posted speed limit. The claim initiates a legal process that may result in penalties, such as fines or points on the driver's license, if the violation is proven or uncontested.

A Full Faith & Credit Clause claim is a legal argument asserting that a law or action violates the Full Faith and Credit Clause of the United States Constitution by failing to give proper recognition and enforcement to the public acts, records, and judicial decisions of other states. This claim challenges the validity of the law or action based on an alleged failure to comply with the constitutional requirement to honor and respect the legal proceedings and official records of other states.

An associational discrimination claim is a legal action brought by an individual who alleges they have been subjected to unfair treatment or adverse actions by an employer or organization based on their association or relationship with a person who has a protected characteristic, such as race, disability, religion, or age. Under anti-discrimination laws like the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, it is unlawful for employers to discriminate against employees due to their association with someone who has a protected status, even if the employee themselves does not possess that status.

A Know Your Customer (KYC) Violation Claim arises when a financial institution or business fails to comply with regulations requiring them to verify the identity of their clients and assess potential risks of illegal intentions for the business relationship. This claim involves allegations of inadequate customer due diligence, failure to maintain proper records, or lack of ongoing monitoring.

It also encompasses the total assets and liabilities left by a person at death, which are subject to distribution according to the terms of a will or the laws of intestacy.
Real estate office buildings.

An Airport - Real Estate Asset refers to the land, buildings, and infrastructure associated with an airport, including runways, terminals, and other facilities. This type of asset involves both operational and non-operational properties that contribute to the airport's functionality and revenue generation.

A Real Estate Operator is a professional or entity responsible for the day-to-day management, maintenance, and operations of real estate properties. This role includes overseeing property functions, ensuring tenant satisfaction, handling leasing activities, and maintaining the physical condition of the property.

Trust and Estate Claims refer to legal actions related to disputes over the distribution of assets and property left behind by a deceased person, including challenges to the validity of wills, allegations of mismanagement by trustees, and interference with expected inheritances.

Estate Planning Objectives refer to the goals and purposes that individuals aim to achieve when organizing their assets, properties, and financial affairs for the future distribution upon their death. These objectives ensure the proper management and transfer of wealth, fulfillment of personal wishes, and minimization of taxes and legal complications.

properties.folio_definition: In legal procedures, "filed" refers to the formal submission of documents, such as lawsuits, motions, or applications, to a court, agency, or other official body. This act initiates or progresses legal proceedings and is a critical step in ensuring that legal matters are officially recorded, recognized, and acted upon according to relevant laws and regulations.

properties.folio_definition: "Reviewed" in a legal setting refers to the examination and evaluation of legal documents, motions, or evidence. properties.folio_definition: In legal contexts, "advised" refers to the provision of professional legal counsel or recommendations to clients, based on the interpretation of the law and its application to specific circumstances. This process is critical in guiding clients through legal complexities, ensuring informed decision-making and compliance with legal norms and obligations.

In the legal arena, "decided" refers to the process of reaching a conclusion or verdict in a legal matter or legal document. This term encompasses various outcomes such as acceptance, affirmation, denial, or dismissal, representing the authoritative resolution or determination made by a court or tribunal.

"Asserted" in a legal context refers to the act of confidently stating or declaring a fact, right, or legal position, typically as part of a legal claim or defense. This assertion forms the basis of a party's argument or claim in legal proceedings, setting the stage for the legal debate and burden of proof. Compared with the more-argumentative terms "opposed" or "supported," the term "asserted" is more neutral.

ruler_concepts.folio_definition: A Judgment is a written decision by a court or other tribunal determining the rights and obligations of the parties involved in a legal dispute, often following a trial or other legal proceeding.
An Order, also known as a Ruling, is a written document issued by a trial court that directs parties to take certain actions or makes a decision on a specific matter in a legal case.
The concept of Disbursement (Distribution) refers to the act of distributing funds or assets to the intended recipients, often as part of a larger event or phase.

An Estate refers to all the possessions, property, and legal rights owned by an individual, including land, tenements, and personal assets. It also encompasses the total assets and liabilities left by a person at death, which are subject to distribution according to the terms of a will or the laws of intestacy.
Dead refers to the state of an individual who has ceased to live and has no vital signs or brain activity.
A petition is a formal written request submitted to a court or other authority, typically seeking a specific legal action or remedy.
The concept of Disbursement (Distribution) refers to the act of distributing funds or assets to the intended recipients, often as part of a larger event or phase.

A central bank is a national financial institution responsible for managing a country's monetary policy, issuing currency, and regulating the banking sector to ensure financial stability. Central banks play a crucial role in controlling inflation, setting interest rates, and serving as a lender of last resort to commercial banks during financial crises.

An Estate refers to all the possessions, property, and legal rights owned by an individual, including land, tenements, and personal assets. It also encompasses the total assets and liabilities left by a person at death, which are subject to distribution according to the terms of a will or the laws of intestacy.

A decedent is a legal term used to refer to a deceased person, particularly in the context of estate planning and probate law. The decedent's estate encompasses all assets, liabilities, rights, and obligations left behind, which are subject to administration and distribution according to the decedent's will or state intestacy laws.

Age refers to the length of time that has elapsed since an individual's birth, typically measured in years, months, or days. In various contexts, age can be a factor in determining discrimination claims, legal rights, responsibilities, and eligibility for certain benefits or programs, such as voting, obtaining a driver's license, accessing healthcare, or participating in educational and employment opportunities.
Inventory refers to the goods or materials that a business holds for the ultimate purpose of resale or production.
Appraisal refers to the process of determining the value of a property or asset, and is a type of event that can occur within the context of litigation or a transaction.
Demand or assertion seeking money, property, or enforcement of another right as provided by law.

The attribute "interested" refers to parties who have a stake in the proceedings, such as creditors, shareholders, or other entities that may be affected by the outcome. Interested counsel specifically denotes legal representatives advocating on behalf of these stakeholders to protect their interests during the process.

The decedent's estate encompasses all assets, liabilities, rights, and obligations left behind, which are subject to administration and distribution according to the decedent's will or state intestacy laws.

Residence refers to a specific place where an individual or entity lives or operates. It can include a personal residence where someone lives, or a business residence where a company operates its activities. This aspect is important for several legal analyses (e.g., personal jurisdiction, bankruptcy law, tax law). A Vacation, or Holiday, is a period of time taken off from work or regular activities for rest, relaxation, or travel. This planned absence from routine duties is typically paid time off for employees, allowing for leisure activities, personal pursuits, or extended breaks from professional responsibilities.

Financial Assets are economic resources that have value and can be owned or controlled with the expectation of generating future income or financial benefits. They can exist in various forms such as cash, stocks, bonds, and real estate.

An investment account is an account held with a financial institution that allows individuals to invest in various financial assets such as stocks, bonds, mutual funds, and ETFs. These accounts are geared towards wealth accumulation and may come with various tax implications.
Northern refers to the region or area located in the northern part of Sudan.
Northern (Zambia) refers to the northern region of the country of Zambia, including its political subdivisions and any associated geographic features.
Northern (Sierra Leone) refers to the northern region of Sierra Leone, which includes multiple districts and is bordered by Guinea to the north and east.

A trust, in estate planning, is a legal entity that holds and manages assets for the benefit of specific individuals or entities, known as beneficiaries. ruler_concepts.folio_definition: Industries in the Credit Intermediation and Related Activities subsector group establishments that (1) lend funds raised from depositors; (2) lend funds raised from credit market borrowing; or (3) facilitate the lending of funds or issuance of credit by engaging in such activities as mortgage and loan brokerage, clearinghouse and reserve services, and check cashing services.

sentence_pos.text:
FINAL JUDGMENT AND ORDER OF DISTRIBUTION

IN THE CIRCUIT COURT OF COOK COUNTY, ILLINOIS
PROBATE DIVISION

In Re: Estate of MARGARET ELEANOR WORTHINGTON, Deceased
Case No. 2023-P-004521

THIS CAUSE came before the Court on the Petition for Final Distribution filed by First National Bank of Chicago, as Executor of the Estate of Margaret Eleanor Worthington ("Decedent"), who died testate on October 12, 2023, at the age of 87, at Northwestern Memorial Hospital, 251 East Huron Street, Chicago, Illinois 60611.

The Court, having reviewed the final accounting, the inventory and appraisal, all claims filed against the estate, and the objections filed by interested parties, and being fully advised in the premises, FINDS as follows:

I. ESTATE ASSETS AND VALUATION

The total gross estate of the Decedent, as determined by the appraiser, Robert J. Flanagan, MAI, of Flanagan & Associates, 180 North LaSalle Street, Suite 3200, Chicago, Illinois 60601, is valued at $14,850,000.00, consisting of:

(a) Real Property:
- Residence at 1440 North Lake Shore Drive, Unit 32B, Chicago, IL 60610 — $3,200,000.00
- Vacation home at 285 Ocean Road, Kennebunkport, Maine 04046 — $1,750,000.00
- Rental property at 742 West Webster Avenue, Chicago, IL 60614 — $825,000.00

(b) Financial Assets:
- JPMorgan Chase & Co. investment account ending in 4589 — $4,250,000.00
- Charles Schwab & Co. brokerage account ending in 7823 — $2,100,000.00
- Northern Trust Company trust account — $1,450,000.00
- Bank of America checking account ending in 3012 — $175,000.00

(c) Personal Property:
- Art collection appraised by Christie's International Real Estate — $850,000.00
- Jewelry appraised by Tiffany & Co. — $250,000.00

II.

DEBTS AND EXPENSES

The following claims have been allowed:
- Northwestern Memorial Hospital — medical expenses: $287,450.00
- Internal Revenue Service — federal estate tax (Form 706): $2,145,000.00
- Illinois Department of Revenue — state estate tax: $412,000.00
- Executor's fees (First National Bank of Chicago): $148,500.00
- Attorney's fees (Winston & Strawn LLP): $95,000.00
- Accounting fees (Deloitte & Touche LLP): $45,000.00
- Funeral and burial expenses: $22,500.00
Total debts and expenses: $3,155,450.00

III.
NET DISTRIBUTABLE ESTATE
The net distributable estate, after payment of all allowed claims and expenses, is $11,694,550.00.
IV.

DISTRIBUTION

Pursuant to the Last Will and Testament of Margaret Eleanor Worthington, executed on June 3, 2019, and admitted to probate on November 15, 2023, the estate shall be distributed as follows:

(a)

To ELIZABETH ANNE WORTHINGTON PRICE, daughter, of 890 Park Avenue, New York, NY 10075 — 40% of the net estate: $4,677,820.00

(b)

To THOMAS JAMES WORTHINGTON, son, of 1200 Pacific Coast Highway, Malibu, CA 90265 — 40% of the net estate: $4,677,820.00

(c) To THE UNIVERSITY OF CHICAGO, for the Margaret Worthington Memorial Scholarship Fund, 5801 South Ellis Avenue, Chicago, IL 60637 — 10% of the net estate: $1,169,455.00

(d) To THE ART INSTITUTE OF CHICAGO, 111 South Michigan Avenue, Chicago, IL 60603 — 5% of the net estate: $584,727.50

(e)

To CATHOLIC CHARITIES OF THE ARCHDIOCESE OF CHICAGO, 721 North LaSalle Drive, Chicago, IL 60654 — 5% of the net estate: $584,727.50

V. ORDER

IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that the Executor shall distribute the estate in accordance with the foregoing findings within sixty (60) days of the date of this Order.
The Executor shall file a receipt of distribution with this Court upon completion.
The claim filed by DAVID MICHAEL WORTHINGTON, nephew, of 445 East Ohio Street, Chicago, IL 60611, contesting the validity of the Will under 755 ILCS 5/4-1, is DENIED.
The Court finds that the Decedent had testamentary capacity and was not subject to undue influence.
See In re Estate of Hoover, 155 Ill. 2d 402 (1993);
In re Estate of Henke, 203 Ill. App.
ENTERED this 1st day of March, 2024.
Hon.

Patricia M. Sullivan
Judge of the Circuit Court of Cook County
Richard J. Daley Center
50 West Washington Street
Chicago, Illinois 60602

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A Judgment is a written decision by a court or other tribunal determining the rights and obligations of the parties involved in a legal dispute, often following a trial or other legal proceeding.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A Summary Judgment is a legal decision made by a judge based on the evidence and facts presented in a case, without the need for a trial.
An Offer of Judgment is a legal document submitted by one party to another during a lawsuit, proposing a settlement amount to resolve the case.
A stipulated judgment is a legal decision agreed upon by both parties involved in a case, which is then approved by a judge.

A judgment lien is a type of lien that is created when a court grants a judgment in favor of a creditor, giving them the right to place a lien on the debtor's property in order to secure payment of the debt.
The Entry of Judgment is a legal event that marks the official recording of a court or arbitral decision, which may include a judgment or a decree.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: An Order, also known as a Ruling, is a written document issued by a trial court that directs parties to take certain actions or makes a decision on a specific matter in a legal case.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A judicial order is a written directive issued by a court, typically in response to a motion or petition, that directs a party to take or refrain from taking a particular action.
A Protective Order is a legal order issued by a court to protect a person or entity from harm or harassment by another party.

An Order for Telephonic Hearing is a written document that directs a court proceeding to be conducted via telephone, videoconference, or other remote means, and is typically used in bankruptcy and restructuring cases.

A Work Order Clause is a provision in a contract that outlines the specific tasks or services to be performed by one party for another, often including details such as timelines, deliverables, and compensation.
A Temporary Restraining Order (TRO) is a provisional legal remedy that prohibits a party from taking certain actions until a court can make a final decision on the matter.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: The concept of Disbursement (Distribution) refers to the act of distributing funds or assets to the intended recipients, often as part of a larger event or phase.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A Mixed Distribution Fund is an investment fund that allocates its investment profits by partially distributing them to shareholders and partially reinvesting them back into the fund. This balanced approach aims to provide both income and capital growth to investors.

Asset Distribution Policy refers to the strategy a fund employs to manage and allocate its investment profits. This policy includes income funds, which regularly distribute profits to investors; accumulation funds, which reinvest profits back into the fund; and mixed funds, which combine both strategies by partially distributing and partially reinvesting profits.

Applicants must meet the qualifications outlined in the NAFTA agreement and provide the necessary documentation to demonstrate eligibility. A freedom to petition claim is a legal action initiated by an individual or group alleging that their right to petition the government for a redress of grievances, as protected by the First Amendment of the United States Constitution, has been violated by the government or a government actor. The First Amendment guarantees that the government shall not abridge the freedom to petition, which includes the right to make requests or complaints to the government, access courts, and participate in other forms of advocacy without fear of retaliation or punishment. A freedom to petition claim may arise in various contexts, such as when a government body enacts a law or regulation that unduly restricts the right to petition, when an individual or group is retaliated against for filing a complaint or lawsuit, or when access to legal processes or governmental decision-making is improperly denied.

The Farm Service Agency (FSA) is the United States Department of Agriculture agency into which were merged several predecessor agencies, including the Agricultural Stabilization and Conservation Service (ASCS). The ASCS was, as the FSA is now, primarily tasked with the implementation of farm conservation and regulation laws around the country.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in creating advertising campaigns and placing such advertising in print and digital periodicals, newspapers, radio and television, or other media. These establishments are organized to provide a full range of services (i.e., through in-house capabilities or subcontracting), including advice, creative services, account management, production of advertising content, media planning, and buying (i.e., placing advertising).
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in purchasing advertising time or space from media outlets and reselling it to advertising agencies or individual companies directly.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Inventory refers to the goods or materials that a business holds for the ultimate purpose of resale or production.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Bankruptcy Schedule - Property (Schedule A/B) is a form in bankruptcy filings that requires the debtor to list all real and personal property they own, including their estimated value. This schedule provides a comprehensive inventory of the debtor's assets, helping the court and creditors assess the debtor's financial situation and available resources for repayment.

A Catalogue is a type of directory that systematically lists, describes, and often categorizes items or services, typically for the purpose of identification, reference, or purchase. Unlike a simple directory, a catalogue often includes additional information about the listed items, such as specifications, prices, or images.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Appraisal refers to the process of determining the value of a property or asset, and is a type of event that can occur within the context of litigation or a transaction.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Events occurring during the course of disputes.
Appraisal Management refers to the process of overseeing and coordinating the evaluation of an individual's performance or value, within the context of managing people.

The right of a shareholder to demand fair value for their shares in a corporation in the event of certain major corporate actions, such as mergers or acquisitions, as specified in the Conditions to Closing.

Conditions to Closing refer to the specific requirements and obligations that must be fulfilled by parties involved in a merger or acquisition in order for the transaction to be completed, including obtaining clearance from CFIUS, ensuring the absence of litigation, complying with covenants, and providing representations and warranties.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: The attribute "interested" refers to parties who have a stake in the proceedings, such as creditors, shareholders, or other entities that may be affected by the outcome. Interested counsel specifically denotes legal representatives advocating on behalf of these stakeholders to protect their interests during the process.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: The attribute "disinterested" refers to parties, often trustees or professionals, who have no personal interest in the outcome of the proceedings and no prior relationship with the debtor or creditors. Disinterested parties ensure unbiased administration and decision-making, fostering fairness and integrity in the process.
From U.S. Bankruptcy Court PACER

Actor Attributes: Characteristics or qualities that define the role or position of a person involved in a legal matter, including their level of involvement (inside, outside, lead, local, supporting).

An Interest Payment is a periodic payment made by a borrower to a lender as compensation for the use of borrowed funds. This payment is typically a percentage of the principal amount and is agreed upon at the time of the loan agreement, representing the cost of borrowing money.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: An expert who conducts an appraisal.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Industries in the Real Estate group are primarily engaged in renting or leasing real estate to others; managing real estate for others; selling, buying, or renting real estate for others; and providing other real estate related services, such as appraisal services. This subsector includes equity Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) that are primarily engaged in leasing buildings, dwellings, or other real estate property to others.
M&A F6 Real Property Practice refers to the legal work performed by a Legal Services Provider in the course of a merger and acquisition transaction that involves real estate property.

The concept of Trespass to Real Property refers to the intentional and unauthorized entry onto someone else's land or property, causing damage or interference with the owner's right to exclusive possession.

Payment of Recurrent Taxes on Immovable Property refers to the regular, often annual, tax payments made by property owners to local or national governments based on the assessed value of their real estate. These taxes fund public services such as education, infrastructure, and emergency services.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Residence refers to a specific place where an individual or entity lives or operates. It can include a personal residence where someone lives, or a business residence where a company operates its activities. This aspect is important for several legal analyses (e.g., personal jurisdiction, bankruptcy law, tax law). Residence refers to a person's fixed, permanent, and principal home, often with legal implications and the intention to remain for an extended period, in contrast to "occupancy," which refers to the physical act of living in or using a property, which can be temporary or intermittent,

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: The concept of "Personal Jurisdiction by Residence" refers to the authority of a court to hear a case based on the fact that the defendant resides within the court's geographic jurisdiction.

A House is a residential building designed to provide living space for individuals or families, typically featuring multiple rooms, including bedrooms, a kitchen, a bathroom, and common living areas. Houses vary in size, style, and structure, ranging from single-story bungalows to multi-story mansions.

A Mobile Home is a prefabricated residential structure built in a factory and transported to its site, designed for permanent or semi-permanent habitation. These homes are typically placed in mobile home parks or on private land and can be moved to different locations as needed.

Personal Jurisdiction Bases refer to the various legal grounds or criteria that a court may use to establish its authority over a person or entity in a legal matter, such as through property ownership, physical presence, or consent.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A Vacation, or Holiday, is a period of time taken off from work or regular activities for rest, relaxation, or travel. This planned absence from routine duties is typically paid time off for employees, allowing for leisure activities, personal pursuits, or extended breaks from professional responsibilities.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in operating overnight recreational camps, such as children's camps, family vacation camps, hunting and fishing camps, and outdoor adventure retreats, that offer trail riding, white water rafting, hiking, and similar activities. These establishments provide accommodation facilities, such as cabins and fixed campsites, and other amenities, such as food services, recreational facilities and equipment, and organized recreational activities.

Maine State Courts are the judicial system of the state of Maine, consisting of the Maine Superior Court and the Maine Supreme Court.
The Maine Supreme Court is the highest court in the state of Maine and serves as the final court of appeal for legal cases within the state's judicial system.
The Maine Superior Court is a court of general jurisdiction within the Maine State Courts system, with authority to hear civil and criminal cases.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Financial Assets are economic resources that have value and can be owned or controlled with the expectation of generating future income or financial benefits. They can exist in various forms such as cash, stocks, bonds, and real estate.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A Structured Certificate is a financial instrument that combines features of fixed income securities and derivatives to offer customized risk-return profiles based on the performance of underlying assets. It typically provides exposure to various asset classes while offering some form of principal protection or enhanced returns under specific market conditions.

Swaps are derivative contracts through which two parties exchange financial instruments, typically cash flows or other financial assets, based on predetermined conditions. Common types include interest rate swaps, currency swaps, and commodity swaps, each designed to manage risk or achieve specific financial objectives.
Type of resource that is owned or controlled by a person, business, or economic entity

Bond assets refer to debt securities issued by corporations, governments, or other entities that may be part of the target company's financial assets being transferred during the deal. Bonds represent a promise by the issuer to repay the principal amount along with interest to the bondholder at a specified maturity date.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: An investment account is an account held with a financial institution that allows individuals to invest in various financial assets such as stocks, bonds, mutual funds, and ETFs. These accounts are geared towards wealth accumulation and may come with various tax implications.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: An account in a financial context, such as a bank or investment firm, is a contractual arrangement that allows individuals or entities to deposit, withdraw, or manage money and financial assets. Accounts can come in various forms, such as savings accounts, checking accounts, or investment accounts, each with their own rules, benefits, and limitations.
Financial and operational indicators designed to evaluate the performance of engagements or transactions.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in acting as principals (except investment bankers, securities dealers, and commodity contracts dealers) in buying or selling financial contracts generally on a spread basis. Principals are investors that buy or sell for their own account.

A pension account is a financial arrangement where an individual or employee contributes funds during their working years to build a retirement fund. The accumulated funds are invested, and upon retirement, the account provides regular payments or a lump sum to support the retiree's financial needs.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A trust, in estate planning, is a legal entity that holds and manages assets for the benefit of specific individuals or entities, known as beneficiaries. It's established by a grantor (or settlor) and is overseen by a trustee who ensures the assets are managed and distributed according to the grantor's instructions.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A business trust is a form of business organization which is similar to a corporation, in which investors receive transferable certificates of beneficial interest.

A Living Trust, also known as a revocable trust, is a legal entity created during the grantor's lifetime to hold and manage assets. It can be altered, amended, or revoked by the grantor at any time before their death, allowing flexibility in asset management and distribution.

An Irrevocable Trust is a legal entity that, once established, cannot be altered, amended, or revoked by the grantor without the consent of the beneficiaries. Assets transferred into this trust are permanently removed from the grantor's estate, offering potential tax benefits and protection from creditors.
A Deed of Trust is a legal agreement that transfers the title of real property to a trustee as security for a loan between a borrower and a lender.

Trust and Estate Claims refer to legal actions related to disputes over the distribution of assets and property left behind by a deceased person, including challenges to the validity of wills, allegations of mismanagement by trustees, and interference with expected inheritances.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A bank is a financial institution licensed to receive deposits, make loans, and provide various financial services such as wealth management, currency exchange, and safe deposit boxes. Banks play a crucial role in the economy by facilitating financial transactions, providing credit, and offering investment products.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: The Export/Import Bank of the United States (abbreviated as Ex-Im Bank or the Bank) is the official export credit agency (ECA) of the United States federal government. Operating as a wholly owned federal government corporation, the Bank "assists in financing and facilitating U.S. exports of goods and services".
Bank Mishandling Funds Transfer refers to a legal claim made by a creditor against a bank for improperly handling the transfer of funds, which may involve commercial paper and banking regulations.

When a bank fails to complete a funds transfer initiated by a customer before the customer closes their account or withdraws their authorization, resulting in the funds not being transferred, and potentially causing financial harm to the customer.

_backup_candidates.folio_label: Bank’s Wrongful Payment of Negotiable Instrument
_backup_candidates.folio_definition: The Bank's Wrongful Payment of Negotiable Instrument refers to a situation where a bank makes a payment on a negotiable instrument without proper authorization or without verifying the authenticity of the instrument, resulting in a loss to the rightful owner of the instrument.

_backup_candidates.folio_label: Bank Misconduct When Relying on Power of Attorney
_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Bank Misconduct When Relying on Power of Attorney: The wrongful or illegal actions taken by a bank when it relies on a power of attorney granted by a customer to conduct transactions on their behalf, often resulting in harm or loss to the customer.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A checking account is a deposit account that allows for frequent transactions, including deposits and withdrawals. It is commonly used for daily expenses and may come with a debit card, checks, and online banking features.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: An account in a financial context, such as a bank or investment firm, is a contractual arrangement that allows individuals or entities to deposit, withdraw, or manage money and financial assets. Accounts can come in various forms, such as savings accounts, checking accounts, or investment accounts, each with their own rules, benefits, and limitations.
Financial and operational indicators designed to evaluate the performance of engagements or transactions.

A Payable-on-Death (POD) Account is a financial account, such as a bank account or a certificate of deposit, that allows the account holder to designate a specific beneficiary who will receive the funds in the account upon the account holder's death. This designation operates outside of the probate process, making it a straightforward way to transfer assets to a chosen beneficiary without the need for a will or going through the probate court.
A European Unit of Account 9 (E.U.A.-9) is a bond market unit that falls under the Currency category and is used as a standardization of money in the European financial market.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: An Estate refers to all the possessions, property, and legal rights owned by an individual, including land, tenements, and personal assets. It also encompasses the total assets and liabilities left by a person at death, which are subject to distribution according to the terms of a will or the laws of intestacy.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: The sale of personal property refers to the transfer of ownership of tangible items that are not considered goods under the law, such as vehicles or furniture, in exchange for payment.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: The concept of Trespass to Personal Property or Chattel refers to the intentional interference with another person's lawful possession of their personal property or chattel, without their consent.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Art refers to the expression or application of human creative skill and imagination, typically in a visual form such as painting or sculpture, producing works to be appreciated primarily for their beauty or emotional power. It encompasses various mediums, techniques, and styles, which are often subject to cultural and historical context.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in retailing original and limited edition art works created by others. Included in this industry are establishments primarily engaged in displaying works of art for retail sale in art galleries.

The prior art search process is a methodical examination conducted to identify existing knowledge, inventions, or technologies relevant to a new invention or patent application. This process helps determine the novelty and non-obviousness of the invention by uncovering prior art that may impact its patentability.
See industry description for 459920.

The patent assessment process is a systematic investigation conducted to ascertain the novelty and non-obviousness of an invention by examining existing patents and other relevant technical literature. This process is crucial for determining the likelihood of obtaining a patent and avoiding infringement of existing patents.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Debts are financial obligations that require an individual or entity to repay borrowed money or assets to a lender according to agreed-upon terms. These obligations can take various forms, including loans, bonds, or promissory notes, and may accrue interest over time.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A Schedule of Post-Petition Debts is a list of debts incurred by a debtor after filing for bankruptcy, which must be disclosed to the court and creditors.

The concept of Partnership Liability for Debts refers to the legal responsibility of partners in a partnership to pay off any debts incurred by the partnership, even if it means using their personal assets to do so.
Bankruptcy Schedules refer to the required documentation filed by a debtor in a bankruptcy case, detailing their assets, liabilities, income, and expenses.

Corporate and Partnership Liability Claims refer to legal claims made against corporations or partnerships for their actions or inactions that result in harm or injury to others, including claims related to breach of fiduciary duty, wrongful interference, debt liability, freeze-out of minority shareholders, and piercing the corporate veil.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Expenses refer to the various costs incurred during a legal matter, including but not limited to word processing, deposition transcripts, litigation support vendors, meals, copying, experts, trial exhibits, delivery services/messengers, witness fees, local counsel, postage, other professionals, online research, arbitrators/mediators, travel, trial transcripts, other, subpoena fees, outside printing, private investigators, facsimile, and court fees.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Medical Expenses refer to the costs incurred for medical treatment and care, including both past and future expenses, as a result of an injury or illness, and are considered a type of economic damage in legal cases.
Expenses incurred for medical treatment or services that have already been provided to an individual.
Matter Net Expenses refers to the costs incurred by a law firm in providing legal services to a client, which are then passed on to the client as part of the engagement fee.
A payee name associated with expenses incurred during the provision of legal services, distinct from fees charged by the payee for their services.
Matter Tax on Expenses: A specific type of tax detail that pertains to the engagement fee detail and is related to the expenses incurred during the provision of legal services.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Future Medical Expenses: Costs for medical treatment or care that are anticipated to be incurred by an individual at a later time due to an injury or illness.
Expenses incurred for medical treatment or services that have already been provided to an individual.

A Medical Payment Evidentiary Objection is raised when a party in a legal proceeding believes that evidence of payments made for medical expenses is being used to prove liability or fault. This objection aims to prevent the prejudicial impact of such evidence, as the payment of medical bills does not necessarily indicate an admission of liability.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) is the revenue service of the United States federal government. The government agency is a bureau of the Department of the Treasury.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: The Department of the Treasury (USDT) is an executive department and the treasury of the United States federal government.
U.S. Federal agencies includes legislative, judicial and executive brach departments and agencies. It also includes Special Inspector General Office and quasi-official agencies.

The IRS Whistleblower Office is a branch of the United States Internal Revenue Service that will "process tips received from individuals, who spot tax problems in their workplace, while conducting day-to-day personal business or anywhere else they may be encountered."

The U.S. Executive Branch is the division of the federal government responsible for enforcing laws and administering public policy, headed by the President of the United States. It comprises the President, Vice President, Cabinet members, and federal agencies and departments.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A document used to capture information, then convey it (e.g., intake form, court form).

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A Long Form Complaint, also known as a Master Long Form Complaint, is a detailed legal document that outlines the plaintiff's claims and allegations against the defendant in a civil lawsuit.

A Medical Referral Form is a document that a healthcare provider uses to send a patient to another healthcare provider for further examination or treatment. The form typically includes relevant medical history and the reason for the referral.
A Domestic Support Obligation Form is a legal document related to bankruptcy and restructuring that outlines the financial obligations of a debtor to provide support to their dependents.

A Large Trader Identification Form is a filing required by the financial regulator (e.g., U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC)) from large traders, defined as those whose transactions in National Market System (NMS) securities equal or exceed specified thresholds. The form provides the financial regulator with information about the large trader's identity, business activities, and affiliates to enhance market surveillance and analyze the impact of large traders on the securities market.
A short form lease clause is a provision within a contract that outlines the terms and conditions of a lease agreement in a concise manner.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A lawyer is a legal professional who provides legal advice and representation to clients in various legal matters, including litigation, transactions, and regulatory compliance.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A billing attorney is the lawyer responsible for keeping track of billable hours and expenses associated with a legal matter, and for preparing and issuing invoices to clients. This attorney ensures that the client is accurately billed for legal services provided, and may also address any billing inquiries or disputes that may arise.

A responsible attorney is the lawyer who is designated as the primary person in charge of overseeing and managing a specific legal matter or case. This attorney is accountable for the progress, strategy, communication, and overall handling of the case, and may delegate tasks to other attorneys or support staff under their supervision.

A relationship attorney is a lawyer who serves as the primary point of contact and maintains an ongoing relationship with a client on behalf of a law firm. This attorney is responsible for understanding the client's needs, expectations, and preferences, and works to ensure that the firm provides consistent, high-quality legal services tailored to the client's requirements.
The intentional misrepresentation or concealment of facts by an attorney to their client or the court, which constitutes a breach of the attorney's duty of loyalty and candor.

The Attorney-Client Privilege is a legal concept that protects the confidentiality of communications between an attorney and their client, and prevents those communications from being disclosed without the client's consent.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A limited liability partnership (LLP) is a partnership in which some or all partners (depending on the jurisdiction) have limited liabilities. It therefore can exhibit elements of partnerships and corporations. In a LLP, each partner is not responsible or liable for another partner's misconduct or negligence.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A partnership is a type of legal entity where two or more individuals or entities come together to carry out a business venture, sharing profits and losses.

A legal entity is a legal construct through which the law allows a group of natural persons to act as if they were a single person for certain purposes. The most common purposes are lawsuits, property ownership, and contracts. Some examples of legal entities include companies, cooperatives (co-ops), corporations, municipalities, political parties, sovereigns, states, temples, trade unions, and ships or vessels.
This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in retailing paint, wallpaper, and related supplies.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in the merchant wholesale distribution of paints, varnishes, and similar coatings; pigments; wallpaper; and supplies, such as paintbrushes and rollers.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A funeral is a ceremony or service held in honor of a deceased person, often involving rituals, customs, or religious practices. The event can include various elements such as eulogies, the burial or cremation of the body, and gatherings for mourning and remembrance.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Funeral Practices are the various rituals, ceremonies, and customs performed to honor and remember a deceased person. These practices vary greatly across different cultures, religions, and legal systems, often reflecting the beliefs and traditions of the community.
Events occurring during the course of a transaction.

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in preparing the dead for burial or interment and conducting funerals (i.e., providing facilities for wakes, arranging transportation for the dead, selling caskets and related merchandise). Funeral homes combined with crematories are included in this industry.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Burial is the process of placing a deceased person's body into the ground, typically accompanied by a funeral ceremony. It involves legal and cultural practices that vary widely across different societies and legal systems.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Funeral Practices are the various rituals, ceremonies, and customs performed to honor and remember a deceased person. These practices vary greatly across different cultures, religions, and legal systems, often reflecting the beliefs and traditions of the community.
This U.S. industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing burial caskets, cases, and vaults (except concrete).

This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in miscellaneous manufacturing (except medical equipment and supplies, jewelry and silverware, sporting and athletic goods, dolls, toys, games, office supplies, and signs).

This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in miscellaneous manufacturing, such as jewelry and silverware manufacturing, sporting and athletic goods manufacturing, doll, toy, and game manufacturing, office supplies (except paper) manufacturing, sign manufacturing, and all other miscellaneous manufacturing.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: The suffix "III" stands for "Third" and is used after a person's full name to distinguish them as the third generation to bear that name in their family. Typically, it follows a grandfather, father, and son who all share the same name, and it helps in differentiating among them in formal, legal, or financial documents.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Inventory refers to the goods or materials that a business holds for the ultimate purpose of resale or production.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Bankruptcy Schedule - Property (Schedule A/B) is a form in bankruptcy filings that requires the debtor to list all real and personal property they own, including their estimated value. This schedule provides a comprehensive inventory of the debtor's assets, helping the court and creditors assess the debtor's financial situation and available resources for repayment.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A Claims Filing Practice (Bankruptcy) is a legal practice that involves the preparation and submission of claims in a bankruptcy case.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A Release of All Claims Clause is a legal provision that absolves one party from any liability or responsibility for any claims or damages made by the other party, including those that may arise in the future.

_backup_candidates.folio_label: Mutual Release of All Claims Clause
_backup_candidates.folio_definition: A Mutual Release of All Claims Clause is a legal provision that releases both parties from any and all claims against each other, as specified in a contract or agreement.

A Release Clause is a contractual provision that relieves one party from liability or obligation, typically in exchange for a payment or other consideration, and may take various forms such as Release of All Claims Clause, Release of Liability Clause, Release of Security Interest Clause, Release of Obligations Clause, Release and Indemnity Clause, or Release Agreement Clause.
A Mutual Release Clause is a provision in a contract that allows both parties to relinquish any claims against each other, typically in exchange for some form of consideration.
A contract's clauses (e.g., choice of venue, force majeure, disclaimers).

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Motions in Trial Courts are requests made by parties in a legal case to the judge for a specific action or ruling during the trial process.
A motion is a written request made to a court asking for a specific action to be taken in a legal case, such as a request for a ruling or a request for evidence to be excluded.

Objections to Cross-Examination are formal challenges raised by a party in a legal proceeding, questioning the propriety, relevance, or admissibility of questions or evidence presented during the cross-examination of a witness. These objections aim to ensure the integrity and fairness of the examination process and protect the witness from unfair or improper questioning.

Objections to Direct Examination are formal challenges raised by a party in a legal proceeding, questioning the propriety, relevance, or admissibility of questions or evidence presented during the direct examination of a witness. These objections aim to ensure the integrity and fairness of the examination process.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: Asset Valuation refers to the process and set of methods used to determine the fair market value of an individual asset, such as real estate, stocks, or machinery. This valuation serves as a foundational element in various financial activities, including transactions, balance sheet reporting, and strategic planning

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Asset Valuation Practice refers to the specialized area of Asset Analysis that involves determining the monetary value of a particular asset or group of assets.

Asset Analysis is the process of evaluating and examining the value and condition of assets in the context of a regulatory, bankruptcy or financial restructuring matter, including asset valuation, due diligence, investigation, turnover, recovery, and financial advisory practices.
Any process that involves the liquidation or restructuring a business. The process usually has several parties - debtors, creditors and buyers.
Financial and operational indicators designed to evaluate the performance of engagements or transactions.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: A decedent is a legal term used to refer to a deceased person, particularly in the context of estate planning and probate law. The decedent's estate encompasses all assets, liabilities, rights, and obligations left behind, which are subject to administration and distribution according to the decedent's will or state intestacy laws.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: Judgment As a Matter of Law (JAML) is a legal decision made by a judge based on the evidence presented in a case, rather than being left to the jury to decide.

resolved_concepts.folio_definition: An expert who conducts an appraisal.

_backup_candidates.folio_definition: An expert in a monetary areas, including accounting, appraisal, damages, securities, and other areas of finance.
The fields in which a person is an expert.
